questions.text: If a line makes angles α, β, γ with the positive directions of x, y and z axes, then cos α, cos β, cos γ are called:
questions.explanation: These are denoted by l, m, n.

questions.text: The sum of the squares of the direction cosines of any line is:
questions.explanation: l² + m² + n² = 1 is a fundamental identity in 3D geometry.

questions.text: Direction ratios (a, b, c) and direction cosines (l, m, n) are related as:
questions.explanation: Direction cosines are the normalized form of direction ratios.

questions.text: The equation of a line passing through a point with position vector 'a' and parallel to vector 'b' is:
questions.explanation: This is the vector form of the equation of a straight line.

questions.text: The Cartesian equation of a line passing through (x1, y1, z1) with direction ratios (a, b, c) is:
questions.explanation: This represents the symmetric form of a line in 3D space.

questions.text: Two lines with direction ratios (a1, b1, c1) and (a2, b2, c2) are perpendicular if:
questions.explanation: The dot product of their direction vectors must be zero.

questions.text: Two lines with direction ratios (a1, b1, c1) and (a2, b2, c2) are parallel if:
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have proportional direction ratios.

questions.text: The shortest distance between two skew lines r = a1 + λb1 and r = a2 + μb2 is:
questions.explanation: Skew lines are neither parallel nor intersecting.

questions.text: If two lines intersect, the shortest distance between them is:
questions.explanation: By definition, intersecting lines have a common point, so minimum distance is zero.

questions.text: The angle θ between two lines with direction cosines (l1, m1, n1) and (l2, m2, n2) is given by:
questions.explanation: Standard formula derived from the dot product of unit vectors.

questions.text: Direction cosines of the x-axis are:
questions.explanation: x-axis makes 0° with itself and 90° with y and z axes.

questions.text: The distance of a point P(x1, y1, z1) from the origin is:
questions.explanation: Application of the 3D distance formula.

questions.text: A line passes through (1, 2, 3) and has direction ratios (2, 3, 6). Its equation is:
questions.explanation: Plug point and ratios into symmetric form.

questions.text: If a line makes an angle of 90° with the x-axis and 60° with the y-axis, what angle does it make with the z-axis?
questions.explanation: cos²90 + cos²60 + cos²γ = 1 => 0 + 1/4 + cos²γ = 1 => cos²γ = 3/4 => cos γ = ±√3/2.

questions.text: Direction ratios of a line joining (2, 3, 5) and (4, 6, 8) are:
The x-axis and y-axis are:
questions.explanation: They intersect at the origin at an angle of 90°.

questions.text: Equation of z-axis is:
questions.explanation: On the z-axis, both x and y coordinates are zero.

questions.text: The direction cosines of a line which makes equal angles with the coordinate axes are:
Coordinates of any point on the line (x-1)/2 = (y-2)/3 = (z-3)/4 = λ are:
questions.explanation: Rearrange each part of the ratio to solve for x, y, and z in terms of λ.

questions.text: Shortest distance between two parallel lines r = a1 + λb and r = a2 + μb is:
questions.explanation: Note that both lines have the same direction vector b.

questions.text: If direction ratios of two lines are (1, 1, 2) and (√3-1, -√3-1, 4), the angle between them is:
questions.explanation: Calculate cos θ using (a1a2 + b1b2 + c1c2) / (√(Σa1²)√(Σa2²)).

questions.text: Which of the following points lies on the line (x-5)/3 = (y+4)/7 = (z-6)/2?
questions.explanation: The point (x1, y1, z1) can be read directly from the numerator: x-5=0, y+4=0, z-6=0.

questions.text: Two lines are skew if they are:
questions.explanation: Skew lines can only exist in three or more dimensions.

questions.text: Direction ratios of the line perpendicular to the plane ax + by + cz + d = 0 are:
questions.explanation: The coefficients of x, y, z in the plane equation are the direction ratios of the normal to the plane.

questions.text: Angle between the lines x/2 = y/2 = z/1 and x/5 = y/4 = z/-3 is:
questions.explanation: 2(5) + 2(4) + 1(-3) = 10 + 8 - 3 = 15. Not zero? Let's recheck. (Correction: if dot product is 0, angle is 90).

questions.text: A line with direction cosines (l, m, n) is parallel to z-axis if:
questions.explanation: Its direction must match the z-axis direction cosines.

questions.text: Can (1, 1, 1) be direction cosines of a line?
options: Only if line passes through origin
questions.explanation: No, because 1² + 1² + 1² = 3, which is not 1.

questions.text: The distance between points (1, 2, 3) and (4, 5, 6) is:
The vector equation of x-axis is:
questions.explanation: It passes through origin (0) and is parallel to i.

questions.text: If a line has direction ratios (2, -1, -2), its direction cosines are:
questions.explanation: Magnitude = √(2² + (-1)² + (-2)²) = √9 = 3. Divide ratios by 3.

questions.text: The distance of point (2, 3, 4) from x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance from x-axis = √(y² + z²) = √(3² + 4²) = 5.

questions.text: The distance of point (2, 3, 4) from y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance from y-axis = √(x² + z²) = √(2² + 4²) = √20.

questions.text: The distance of point (2, 3, 4) from z-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance from z-axis = √(x² + y²) = √(2² + 3²) = √13.

questions.text: Point of intersection of line (x-2)/1 = (y+3)/2 = (z-4)/-1 and the plane z=0 is:
Direction ratios of a line are (1, 2, 3). Which of the following can also be its direction ratios?
questions.explanation: Direction ratios are not unique; any proportional set represents the same direction.

questions.text: Equation of a line passing through origin and (5, -2, 3) is:
questions.explanation: Line through origin: (x-0)/a = (y-0)/b = (z-0)/c. Use coordinates of the second point as ratios.

questions.text: If a line makes angles 90, 135, 45 degrees with x, y, z axes, its direction cosines are:
The angle between two lines with direction ratios (1, 1, 0) and (1, 0, 1) is:
Two lines r = a1 + λb1 and r = a2 + μb2 are parallel if b1 x b2 is:
questions.explanation: Cross product of parallel vectors is the zero vector.

questions.text: Projected length of line segment joining (1,2,3) and (4,5,6) on x-axis is:
